subject: Science
chapter_name: The Human Eye and the Colourful World
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze eye optics quantitatively including lens power and accommodation range
Solve numerical problems on vision defects and lens corrections
Derive and apply minimum deviation in prisms; estimate refractive index
Critically evaluate atmospheric refraction and scattering models with evidence
Apply wavelength-dependent scattering law and analyze environmental impacts
Develop higher-order skills: data interpretation, error analysis, interdisciplinary links

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Eye Optics and Accommodation
topics_covered: Detailed eye optics: reduced eye model, focal lengths
Power of accommodation calculation, age effects
Persistence of vision and optical illusions
teaching_strategy: Use optical bench simulation if available
student_activities: Calculate accommodation power for given near/far points
Analyze limitations of simple eye model

lesson_plan.title: Vision Defects – Numerical Corrections and Analysis
topics_covered: Myopia, hypermetropia, presbyopia – quantitative ray analysis
Lens formula application (1/f = 1/v - 1/u), power in dioptres
Bifocals, contact lenses, LASIK – pros/cons evaluation
student_activities: Draw detailed ray diagrams with focal points

lesson_plan.title: Prism Refraction – Quantitative Dispersion Study
topics_covered: Angle of deviation formula, minimum deviation derivation
student_activities: Measure i and δ; plot graph, find δ_m

lesson_plan.title: Atmospheric Refraction – Critical Analysis
topics_covered: Quantitative explanation of time shifts in sunrise/sunset
Twinkling: turbulence effects, stars vs planets comparison
student_activities: Calculate approximate angular shift for sunrise
Analyze why planets twinkle less (disk source)
Write critical note on observational evidence

lesson_plan.title: Scattering – Mathematical and Environmental Insights
topics_covered: Pollution, haze, and enhanced scattering effects
student_activities: Calculate relative scattering blue vs red light
Analyze pollution data impact on sky color

lesson_plan.title: Synthesis, Advanced Problems, and Evaluation
topics_covered: Modern applications: optical fibres, climate optics
student_activities: Critically evaluate a claim (e.g., pollution beauty vs harm)

teaching_aids: Prism, lenses set, laser pointer, milk solution
teacher_tip: Push quantitative rigor—encourage calculator use for realism. Link concepts to JEE/NEET-style questions and current issues (light pollution, corrective tech). Foster debate and evidence-based arguments for deeper understanding.
